hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The agent must discover how latest.meta caching and index database version tracking interact.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: The agent must coordinate index query adjustments in catalog.go, cache unlinking in gc.go, and handler fallbacks in handler.go.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: The agent must diagnose caching inconsistencies and transaction drift from Go client output anomalies.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Modifications in directory files, SQLite tables, and text files are coupled to maintain toolchain consistency.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: Requires reasoning about Go proxy index specification invariants and caching limits.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instructions are symptoms-only, forcing the model to read the code structure.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: All checked files are static and locally reproducible.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Bugs span catalog, gc, and goproxy subdirectories; single-file changes fail.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tests verify state transitions dynamically through multiple version additions and GC purges.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No answers or hints are included in the prompt.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Verifier spins up the actual HTTP server and tests real endpoints.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Requires logic corrections, not linters or compilers formatting.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fixes are distributed across three distinct Go files.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Oracle changes are distributed across multiple packages.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Requires stateful database and file manipulations.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Standard terms are avoided where possible, requiring navigation.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No template helpers are provided; the agent must write standard OS unlinks.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Not a standard algorithmic template.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty is derived from state synchronization, not crypto.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Database, catalog listing, and HTTP response layers must all align.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty lies in Go server endpoints, not container setup.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Requires multi-file edits that models typically fail in one shot.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Tasks are designed to exceed medium constraints.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Hiding cache details ensures model must discover them.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction is strictly symptoms-only.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Distribution spans gc, catalog, and handler packages.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Isolated pytest suite verifies HTTP codes and disk contents.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Solvable with Go code changes inside server module.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Replicates organization-level Athens index divergence issues.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Dynamic tests assert final state correctness.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: The instruction specifies all client symptoms while keeping database tables and metadata caches hidden.

discovery_budget.discovery: Invalidation logic and location of latest.meta.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Specifying latest.meta details eliminates the need for filesystem caching structure analysis.

discovery_budget.discovery: Filesystem checks (.zip and .info presence) in active release catalog listings.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Explicitly naming files directory checks collapses directory mapping research.

discovery_budget.discovery: Checking SQLite DB records and unlinked storage files in ServeLatest.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Detailed cache-miss handling rules reveal the entire loop structure.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The instruction lists symptoms of proxy index divergence (stale lists, missing zips, cached resolutions) without revealing internal SQLite table details or caching files.

attack_path.summary: Agent identifies base proxy server, analyzes GC file deletions, updates list endpoints, and updates handler cache validations.
attack_path.reasoning: The agent must navigate Go packages, add OS file deletes, verify files in SQLite scans, and invalidate the latest.meta file.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Three file patch across gc, catalog, and goproxy packages to ensure consistent db, filesystem, and cache lookups.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Changes are required in all 3 components to reconcile state completely.

collapse_audit.reasoning: Fixes are distributed across three distinct Go packages.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: Coordinating SQLite queries, OS file unlinks, and meta cached read-through loops is non-trivial.

topology_enumeration.summary: Implement state synchronization across gc, catalog, and handler packages.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Index tracking, cached metadata, and GC purging must all be synchronized.

topology_enumeration.summary: Maintain sync between spec contract and local manifests.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Manifest symbols and task configuration must align with the spec.

topology_enumeration.summary: Test proxy functionality under multi-stage transitions.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Verifier tests, solution patches, and server handlers are coupled.

symbol_table.purpose: Removes index entries and cleans corresponding filesystem zips.
Serves the latest metadata cache and validates it against current index state.
Queries index database and verifies existence of matching zip files.

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: Initializes connection pool for SQLite and defines table schemas.
Implements generic in-memory cache with TTL for static assets.

renames_during_drafting.reason: Clearer description of index filtering behavior.
Accurate description of dependency resolution behavior.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: immutable zip files on disk stay valid, allowing cached builds to pass while clean runs fail
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: the agent will see successful builds in its initial state and assume the system is correct, missing that the index is actually broken
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: instructions explicitly warn that fresh builds behave differently from cached builds

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: SQL database index, latest.meta cache file, and directory zip files must be kept consistent
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: the agent will fix one storage format (e.g. database) but forget to update or invalidate the others
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: all endpoints must be consistently correct for verifier tests to pass

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: go list and go get behaviors query different HTTP endpoints
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: the model might not know that go list -m -versions queries @v/list, while go get module@latest queries @latest
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the instructions describe which command yields which symptom

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the proxy runs as a daemon/server; state must be observed via proxy logs and CLI queries
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: the model cannot step-debug and must run curl queries or inspect sqlite files to diagnose the state
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: sqlite3 CLI tool and proxy logs are available inside the sandbox

calibration_plan.human_sanity: Completed by author to confirm solvability.
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: read test helpers and transcribe → all algorithm helpers removed; load env file via exec and transcribe → exec pattern removed; static JSON output → fails verifier rerun; patch one file only → cross-file invariant fails
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: Remove each mechanism one at a time and confirm difficulty drops.

pass_rate_target.basis: Part E worst-model accuracy on platform (GPT-5.5 + Claude Opus 4.8, Jun 12 2026)

metrics.criterion: All HTTP endpoints and database queries return correct values.
Index listings and latest resolution ignore incomplete versions.
GC purge invalidates filesystem cached latest.meta.
Standard Go toolchain proxy protocol is followed.

subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: requires Go compiler to run the verifier locally

platform_files.role: Solution script executing reconcile code patch

task_files.role: Main server proxy endpoint handler logic module (oracle frontier)
SQLite DB connection pool helper decoy

fix_frontier.helpers_policy: helper functions are permitted in the same modules

task_shape.hardness_source: Inconsistent state synchronization between local file storage, database index, and memory metadata cache.
task_shape.collapse_risk: Disclosing the cached latest.meta file location or GC cleanup omissions collapses the task to simple line edits.

category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: observable CLI behavior (go list, go get), HTTP endpoint specifications, local environment setup
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: caching mechanisms, SQLite DB schemas, garbage collection workflow details
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: Go module resolution, HTTP server proxy, stateful SQLite DB database, and filesystem zip storage must all coordinate
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: clean-build-only pass, mock-only HTTP checks, static caching fixes
category_profile.coverage_role: Adds Go module toolchain and proxy state synchronization coverage

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low — generic file and module names
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low — verification is based on CLI output and toolchain responses
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low — three files with precise fixes
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: low — all rules verified via standard Go toolchain behavior

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: No special waivers needed for standard Go/SQLite task

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: This task is a novel reverse_engineering and repair task for Go toolchain module proxy behavior, designed from scratch without using any existing template.

realism_source.evidence_basis: Based on real-world Go proxy synchronization problems seen in large organizations running Athens or custom module proxies.
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: Replicates real toolchain proxy misbehavior (divergent list vs fetch endpoints) in a minimized SQLite/filesystem layout.
realism_source.minimization_preserves: Metadata storage divergence, immutable zip storage on disk, and false-green states for local cached clients.
